facts_disclosed: Bottled sauce line dead — food safety certification costs 180,000 baht and requires factory kitchen

turns.content: ugh today is one of those days na ka. I feel like giving up on something and I need someone to tell me it is okay to give up 😩
Oh no, that doesn't sound great. What happened? Sometimes walking away from something is the smart move, not a failure. Tell me what's going on.

okay so you know my bottled sauce plan?? I was SO excited about it, 4 flavours all tested and ready. I thought it would be easy — just make sauce, put in bottle, sell. but I went to the FDA office to ask about food safety certification and I almost fall off my chair na ka
Thailand's FDA requirements for packaged food products can be intense. What did they tell you? Was it the costs, the facility requirements, or both?

BOTH na ka!! the certification alone cost 180,000 baht!! arai wa!! and that is not even the worst part — they say I cannot make the sauce at my cart or at home. I need a FACTORY KITCHEN. proper commercial kitchen with special equipment, separate storage, temperature control rooms. like I am Nestle or something!! I sell chicken rice on the street I don't have a factory 😭

180,000 baht for certification PLUS a factory kitchen rental — that's a massive investment for a sauce line that hasn't proven market demand yet. Even renting time in a shared commercial kitchen that meets FDA standards isn't cheap. You'd be looking at maybe 20,000-30,000 baht a month on top of the certification. Did you calculate what it would take to break even?

yes I calculate everything na ka. certification 180,000, factory kitchen rent, bottles, labels, shipping... I would need to sell maybe 3,000 bottles before I even break even. and who know if people buy?? my sauce is good at the cart but bottled sauce is a completely different market. the bottled sauce is DEAD. not happening. forget about it 💀

Honestly? That sounds like a really smart decision, not a defeat. You did the research, ran the numbers, and made a rational call instead of throwing money at a dream and hoping it works. A lot of people would have gone ahead and regretted it. What's the plan now — just focusing on what's already working?

yes na ka. just focus on the cart and Instagram. that is enough for now. I was sad all day thinking I am a quitter but the numbers just don't make sense. and honestly running the cart already take all my energy. I wake up 4am every day. when would I even go to a factory kitchen and make sauce?? I was dreaming too big haha

You're not a quitter — you're a realist who did her homework. There's a big difference between giving up because something is hard and walking away because the math doesn't work. You did the second one. And you're right about time — you're running a full operation from 4am. Adding a whole separate production line would spread you way too thin.

Your sauce is still your competitive advantage though. People come to the cart FOR that sauce. You just serve it fresh instead of in a bottle.

haha you know what, yes!! my sauce is special BECAUSE you can only get it at my cart. maybe that is actually better for business na ka. make it exclusive. 'you want Pan's sauce? come to the cart!' okay I feel better now. no more sauce dream, just CART DREAM. my cart is my baby and that is enough 🙏

That's actually a brilliant reframe — exclusivity is a real marketing angle. "You can only get this sauce at the cart" makes people want to come find you. Some of the best food in Bangkok works exactly that way.

And don't feel bad about venting. You had a big plan that didn't work out. But you processed it, made a decision, and already found the positive spin. That's entrepreneur energy. Go sell some khao man gai and forget about the FDA lady. 🙏
haha YES forget the FDA lady!! 😂 thank you na ka you really help me feel better today. okay going to prep for tomorrow now. 4am coming fast!! 🙏🙏
Anytime! And next time you feel like a quitter, remember: quitters don't wake up at 4am six days a week. You're the opposite of a quitter. Go prep and get some rest — goodnight! 🙏
